title: [Bug]: --detach-keys does not work without --tty
body:
### Issue Description

I start rootful podman-4.3.2-dev with:

> \# podman run -d --cap-add=IPC_LOCK --log-driver=none --net=host --read-only --read-only-tmpfs=false --mount type=bind,src=/etc/Z,dst=/conf --rm=true -a=stderr -a=stdout --userns=auto:size=10 localhost/Z:2023-01-14

I only want to read the output of the container, I do not want to send signals to the container, or send data to it.

Then I attach to it with any of
```console
# podman attach -l --detach-keys "c"
# podman attach -l --detach-keys "C"
# podman attach -l --detach-keys "ctrl-c"
# podman attach -l --detach-keys "ctrl-C"
Error: attaching to container d08ef5139377473f832b0d771db95ec5ef45f32e919b06a93f8908932eb599a8: invalid detach keys: Unknown character: 'ctrl-C'
```
and press `c` or `C` after the attachment completes, `podman attach` does not disconnect (detach). Likewise for ctrl-c.  The default `ctrl-p,ctrl-q` also does not work.

When `podman run --tty` then detach-keys does work, the implicit ctrl+p,ctrl+q is also functions.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

See above

### Describe the results you received

Container is not detached on any key combiation, when `--tty` is not passed to `podman run`.

### Describe the results you expected

I expect that `podman attach --detach-keys C` always detaches, irrespective on whether `--tty` is set.  While I have no problem of passing `--tty`, I do not need it, except for handing the detach key combination.

comments.body: I think that is expected, detach-key is meaningful only when a terminal is used

It is not expected.  I have a container which generates output (stdout).  Most of the time I am not interested in the output.  But sometimes I ATTACH to the container to read the output.  I cannot detach, since detach-keys do no work.

That said, `podman attach` does work as expected, but there is no way to detach from the container after `podman attach` is called.  This is not expected.

Closing the issue, I've verified it works in the same way on Docker.

```
# docker run --name foo --rm -i alpine sleep 100
^Z^Z^Z^Z^Z^Z^Z^Z^Z^Z^Z
```

I would also like if this worked. Must this be (usability) bug-compatible to docker?

FWIW, killing the attach process seems to not kill the container.
This is apparently explained already in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/6990#issuecomment-911696145
